record.field: Authelia is a self-hosted authentication and authorization server that adds single sign-on and multi-factor authentication to your applications through a single login portal.

Authelia is a self-hosted authentication and authorization server that adds single sign-on and multi-factor authentication to your applications through a single login portal.

It fulfills the identity and access management (IAM) role for your infrastructure, acting as a companion to reverse proxies that allow, deny, or redirect requests based on per-rule access policies. As an OpenID Certified OpenID Connect 1.0 provider, it integrates cleanly with proxies such as Traefik, nginx, Caddy, HAProxy, and Envoy, and protects both modern apps and legacy services that have no authentication of their own. It is lightweight, written in Go and React, and designed with a security-by-design philosophy.

Key features
  • OpenID Connect 1.0 / OAuth 2.0 provider (OpenID Certified) for app-native SSO
  • Multi-factor authentication: FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys and passkeys, TOTP, and Duo push
  • Fine-grained access control rules by domain, path, method, user, group, and network
  • User backends via file or LDAP/Active Directory, with self-service password reset
  • Reverse-proxy integration with Traefik, nginx, Caddy, HAProxy, Envoy, and Skipper
  • High-availability deployments backed by an external database and Redis sessions
Best for

Teams and self-hosters who want to centralize authentication, enforce MFA, and put SSO in front of multiple web services behind a reverse proxy, without relying on a third-party identity provider.

services: authelia: image: authelia/authelia:latest restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./authelia_config:/config - ../files/configuration.yml:/config/configuration.yml:ro - ../files/users_database.yml:/config/users_database.yml environment: AUTHELIA_JWT_SECRET: $JWT_SECRET AUTHELIA_SESSION_SECRET: $SESSION_SECRET AUTHELIA_STORAGE_ENCRYPTION_KEY: $STORAGE_ENCRYPTION_KEY AUTHELIA_STORAGE_POSTGRES_PASSWORD: $POSTGRES_PASSWORD depends_on: redis: condition: service_healthy postgres: condition: service_healthy ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:9091' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./redis_data:/data command: redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning --requirepass $REDIS_PASSWORD environment: REDIS_PASSWORD: $REDIS_PASSWORD healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - --raw - incr - ping interval: 10s timeout: 3s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal postgres: image: postgres:16-alpine restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_DB: authelia POSTGRES_USER: authelia POSTGRES_PASSWORD: $POSTGRES_PASSWORD healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U authelia -d authelia interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0

############################################################### # Authelia configuration # ###############################################################
# DEFAULT ADMIN CREDENTIALS: # Username: (auto-generated, check users_database.yml) # Password: AdminPass123! # Email: (auto-generated) # # IMPORTANT: Change the password after first login! # SECURITY NOTE: This template starts with one-factor auth for easier setup. # After configuring SMTP/notifications, change the policy to 'two_factor'
# Server Configuration server: address: 'tcp://0.0.0.0:9091' headers: csp_template: ''
# Log Configuration log: level: info format: text
# Theme theme: auto
# TOTP Configuration totp: disable: false issuer: authelia.com algorithm: sha1 digits: 6 period: 30 skew: 1 secret_size: 32
# WebAuthn/FIDO2 Configuration webauthn: disable: false timeout: 60s display_name: Authelia attestation_conveyance_preference: indirect user_verification: preferred
# NTP Configuration ntp: address: 'time.cloudflare.com:123' version: 4 max_desync: 3s disable_startup_check: false disable_failure: false
# Authentication Backend Configuration authentication_backend: password_reset: disable: false custom_url: '' refresh_interval: 5m file: path: /config/users_database.yml watch: false search: email: false case_insensitive: false password: algorithm: argon2 argon2: variant: argon2id iterations: 3 memory: 65536 parallelism: 4 key_length: 32 salt_length: 16
# Password Policy password_policy: standard: enabled: false min_length: 8 max_length: 0 require_uppercase: true require_lowercase: true require_number: true require_special: true zxcvbn: enabled: false min_score: 3
# Session Configuration session: name: authelia_session domain: ${main_domain} same_site: lax secret: ${session_secret} expiration: 1h inactivity: 5m remember_me_duration: 1M redis: host: redis port: 6379 password: ${redis_password} database_index: 0 maximum_active_connections: 8 minimum_idle_connections: 0
# Storage Configuration storage: encryption_key: ${storage_encryption_key} postgres: host: postgres port: 5432 database: authelia schema: public username: authelia password: ${postgres_password} timeout: 5s
# Notifier Configuration notifier: disable_startup_check: true filesystem: filename: /config/notification.txt
# Regulation Configuration regulation: max_retries: 3 find_time: 10m ban_time: 12h
# Access Control Configuration - MODIFIED FOR EASIER INITIAL SETUP access_control: default_policy: deny rules: - domain: ${main_domain} policy: one_factor # Changed from one_factor to two_factor for production

############################################################### # Users Database # ###############################################################
# DEFAULT LOGIN CREDENTIALS: # Username: (generated from username helper) # Password: AdminPass123! # Email: (generated from email helper) # # IMPORTANT: Change the default password after first login! # To generate a new password hash, run: # docker run authelia/authelia:latest authelia hash-password 'your-new-password'
users: ${admin_username}: disabled: false displayname: "Authelia Admin" password: "${admin_password_hash}" email: ${admin_email} groups: - admins - dev
